steps.title: Find the side length
steps.content:
An equilateral triangle has $3$ equal sides. Divide the perimeter by $3$.
$$\frac{672}{3}=224$$

steps.title: Use the height formula
steps.content:
The height of an equilateral triangle with side length $s$ is $\frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2}$. Substitute $s=224$.
$$\frac{224\sqrt{3}}{2}=112\sqrt{3}$$
Since the height is $k\sqrt{3}$, $k=112$. The answer is 112.
